title: Automatically Tracing Referral Chains in Your CRM
excerpt: I record leads in my CRM and assign each one to a lead source. Sometimes, that source is an individual. In such cases, I want to track this person across all of my CRM boards, or at least have a dedicated board that clea…

posts.author: ID37 (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml:
I record leads in my CRM and assign each one to a lead source. Sometimes, that source is an individual. In such cases, I want to track this person across all of my CRM boards, or at least have a dedicated board that clearly shows the entire referral chain.

My goal is to automatically identify the original referrer of any given lead. For example:

  • “Count von Count” (Graf Zahl) only ended up in the system thanks to “Bert,” even though the direct entry came from “Cookie Monster,” who was referred by “Ernie,” who in turn was originally referred by “Bert.”

To achieve this, I’m planning to add an extra field in my CRM, such as “Referred by,” to record who passed along the lead. For instance:

  • Count von Count → Referred by: Cookie Monster
  • Cookie Monster → Referred by: Ernie
  • Ernie → Referred by: Bert

This way, I can trace back through the chain to the original referrer—in this case, Bert.

Has anyone here dealt with similar referral chain setups in monday? Any tips, experiences, or best practices would be greatly appreciated!

posts.author: DavidatWISE (David R Currie)
posts.cookedHtml: I think if the connect boards two way connection feature worked better for connecting a board with itself, it would be great. If I create a two way connect board column, it does not create the inverse connection in the board. I just get one column that connects the two items together, but I cannot see the direction of the referral “Referred To” or “Referred By”. If they created a separate Inverse connection column, I could do this. So if I enter the Referred By for Contact 2 as Contact 1, Contact 1 would see Contact 2 in the Referred By column. So regardless of which column you enter into, it would show up in the inverse column in the other contact.
